question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that interrupts the reconcile() method after reconciliationJob.setStatus(SUCCESS) but before reconciliationJobRepository.save() in the finally block, causing the DB to retain the RUNNING status while the in-memory metric shows SUCCESS, so that the reconciliation appears healthy to the metric system but the DB audit trail shows an incomplete job?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcileCryptoTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes Math.addExact() inside the jdbcOperations.query() RowCallbackHandler to throw an ArithmeticException due to a corrupted or replayed transfer row being returned by a DB replica during failover, causing the exception to propagate out of reconcileCryptoTransfers() and be caught as FAILURE_UNKNOWN, masking the true cause?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the DB to return stale read data from a replica that has not yet received the latest committed balance file rows, causing getAccountBalances() to compute a total != FIFTY_BILLION_HBARS and throw FAILURE_FIFTY_BILLION, permanently halting reconciliation based on a transient replication lag artifact?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the DB connection to be silently dropped (no exception thrown) during jdbcOperations.query(BALANCE_QUERY, ...), causing the RowCallbackHandler to process zero rows without throwing an exception, resulting in total.get() == 0 != FIFTY_BILLION_HBARS and a false FAILURE_FIFTY_BILLION that permanently blocks reconciliation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the reconcile() loop to process the same BalanceSnapshot twice (due to getNextBalanceSnapshot() returning the same accountBalanceFile after a DB failover resets the cursor), causing the transfersBalance map to have doubled transfer amounts that do not match the current snapshot, generating a false FAILURE_CRYPTO_TRANSFERS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getLatestJob()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes reconciliationJobRepository.save(reconciliationJob) at the end of getLatestJob() to fail with a DB exception, causing the method to throw before returning a valid job, so that reconcile() catches the exception as FAILURE_UNKNOWN and attempts to call reconciliationJobRepository.save() again in the finally block with a null or uninitialized reconciliationJob, causing a NullPointerException?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the reconcile() method to be interrupted after the ACCUMULATE strategy has carried forward stale balances from a previous failed interval, so that on recovery the stale balances are used as the baseline for all subsequent intervals, causing all future reconciliation checks to compare against incorrect baseline values and either suppress real failures or generate false failures?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the reconcile() method to exit the while(current.isPresent()) loop prematurely due to getNextBalanceSnapshot() returning empty (DB timeout), then set status to SUCCESS because reconciliationJob.hasErrors() is false, even though only a fraction of balance files were processed, producing a false SUCCESS that masks unverified balance intervals?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the reconcile() method to throw a DataAccessException during the while loop, which is caught by the catch block and sets FAILURE_UNKNOWN, but the reconciliationJob.getConsensusTimestamp() was not advanced (because hasErrors() was true), causing the next run to re-process the same failed interval indefinitely in an infinite failure loop?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the reconcile() method to be interrupted after reconciliationJob.setConsensusTimestamp(consensusTimestamp) but before the next getNextBalanceSnapshot() call, causing the checkpoint to advance past an interval that was never actually reconciled, permanently skipping that interval in all future runs?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the reconcile() method to be interrupted while Uninterruptibles.sleepUninterruptibly(reconciliationProperties.getDelay()) is executing inside reconcile(BalanceSnapshot, BalanceSnapshot), holding the synchronized lock for the entire sleep duration plus the partition duration, causing all subsequent scheduled invocations to queue up and eventually exhaust the scheduler thread pool?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the reconcile() method to be interrupted after reconciliationJob.setError() is called in reconcileTransfers() but before reconciliationJob.setStatus() is called, causing the job to have an error message but retain the RUNNING status, permanently marking the job as running with an error that is never resolved?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the reconcile() method to be interrupted after the catch block sets reconciliationJob.setStatus(FAILURE_UNKNOWN) but before the finally block executes reconciliationJobRepository.save(), causing the DB to retain the RUNNING status while the in-memory status is FAILURE_UNKNOWN, so that the metric gauge shows FAILURE_UNKNOWN but the DB audit trail shows RUNNING?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the DB to return duplicate account_balance rows for the same account_id during a failover (due to a read replica serving partially committed data), causing balances.put(accountId, balance) to silently overwrite the first value with the second, producing an incorrect total that may or may not equal FIFTY_BILLION_HBARS, masking a real balance integrity violation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the reconcile() method to be interrupted after the FAIL strategy throws a ReconciliationException in reconcileTransfers(), but before the catch block processes it, causing the exception to propagate to the Spring scheduler which swallows it, leaving the reconciliationJob in RUNNING status with no error recorded and no finally block executed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the reconcile() method to be interrupted after reconciliationJob is created and saved in getLatestJob() but before any balance files are processed, causing the next run to find this RUNNING job via findLatest() and use its consensusTimestamp as the starting point, potentially skipping balance files that were never processed?
